SCTransform normalization for the transcript assay

Source: R/generics.R, R/normalization.R
SCTransformTranscripts.Rd

Runs SCTransform on the "transcript" assay of the embedded Seurat object and follows it with RunPCA using a larger number of principal components, giving better isoform-level cluster resolution.

Runs SCTransform on the "transcript" Assay5 of the embedded Seurat object, followed by RunPCA with n_pca_dims components.

Usage

SCTransformTranscripts(object, ...)

# S4 method for class 'MatisseObject'
SCTransformTranscripts(
  object,
  n_pca_dims = 50L,
  vars_to_regress = NULL,
  assay = "transcript",
  verbose = TRUE,
  ...
)

Arguments

object

A MatisseObject with a "transcript" assay. Use CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts or CreateMatisseObject(transcript_counts = ...) to create one.

...

Additional arguments forwarded to SCTransform.

n_pca_dims

Integer. Number of principal components to compute. Default: 50.

vars_to_regress

Character vector of variables to regress out during SCTransform (e.g. "percent.mt"). Default: NULL.

assay

Character. Name of the assay to run SCTransform on. Default: "transcript".

verbose

Logical. Default: TRUE.

Value

The updated MatisseObject.

The updated MatisseObject with:

  • An "SCT" assay (created by SCTransform) in the Seurat object.

  • A "pca" reduction computed on the SCT assay.

Details

SCTransform is better suited than standard log-normalization for transcript-level count data: it corrects for sequencing depth while preserving biological variance. Using a higher number of PCA dimensions (default 50) has been found to resolve more isoform-level clusters than the standard 20.

After this step you can call FindNeighbors, FindClusters, and RunUMAP directly on the MatisseObject via the built-in method forwarding (see MatisseObject-class).

See also

CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts, SCTransform, RunPCA
